key_insight: STRAND reinterprets persistence diagrams as survival data, enabling one coherent representation for hypothesis testing, effect sizes, and stable vectorisation.
engineering_relevance: It gives practitioners a statistically grounded way to compare topological summaries and feed them into machine learning pipelines without switching methods.
method: Treat each persistence value as a time-to-event and build the persistence survival function S(t)=P(p>t).
Use the shared survival representation to derive a non-parametric two-sample test and interpretable effect sizes.
Convert the survival curve into a 1-Wasserstein-stable feature vector for downstream prediction.
evidence_signal: The paper reports calibrated Type I error and high power on synthetic manifolds, plus competitive performance across 14 graph and 3D point-cloud benchmarks and an fMRI connectivity application.
watch_item: Performance depends on having enough diagrams and on careful implementation of the survival-based statistics, especially when comparing complex or small-sample datasets.
paper_title: From Persistence to Survival: Hypothesis Testing, Effect Sizes and Vectorisation for Topological Features
